events.headline: Turn every keynote into a conversation.
events.subheadline: Run live polls, Q&A rankings, and sentiment checks across hundreds of participants — Qesto keeps your audience engaged from opening remarks to closing session.

painPoints.heading: The challenges every event organiser knows

passiveAudiences.title: Passive audiences
passiveAudiences.desc: Attendees scroll their phones while speakers talk to a quiet room. Engagement drops off after the first 10 minutes.

slowFeedback.title: Slow feedback loops
slowFeedback.desc: Post-event surveys arrive too late to act on. You only find out what went wrong after people have left.

silentMajority.title: Silent majority
silentMajority.desc: Only the loudest voices get heard in open Q&A. Most attendees never feel safe enough to share their real opinions.

passive.title: [TODO] Audience attention drops invisibly
passive.desc: [TODO] Engagement falls 20 minutes into a one-way session and the data shows up after the room has emptied.

feedback.title: [TODO] Post-event surveys arrive too late
feedback.desc: [TODO] By the time the recap survey lands, the speaker is gone and next year's brief is already being written.

silent.title: [TODO] Sponsor signal stays hidden
silent.desc: [TODO] Only confident voices reach the microphone. The signals sponsors actually paid for never make it into the recap.

hr.headline: Feel the pulse of your people — not just the data.

fear.desc: [TODO] If the anonymity model is implicit or hidden, participation drops and the answers you do get are sanitized.

fatigue.title: [TODO] Survey fatigue erodes trust
fatigue.desc: [TODO] Long forms with no visible follow-through teach employees the next survey isn't worth opening.

q1.question: [TODO] Can I edit AI-generated questions?
q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. Drafts are fully editable before the session starts and during pacing, including question text, response type, and ordering.

q2.question: [TODO] Does AI Insights call third-party model providers?
q2.answer: [TODO] No. Question generation and theme clustering run on Cloudflare Workers AI inside the same edge network that serves the session — there are no calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, or other third-party LLMs.

q3.question: [TODO] Can I compare themes across sessions?
q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Theme groupings persist with the session record so recurring patterns surface across HR pulses, retros, and recurring workshops.

privacy.headline: Your team's responses stay yours — always.
privacy.subheadline: Anonymity modes, GDPR consent logging, and on-device AI inference mean participants can speak honestly and you can prove it. No third-party model providers ever see your data.

q1.question: [TODO] Can we run this without physical sticky notes?
q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. The flow is fully digital from divergent ideation through convergent ranking — and the outputs survive the handoff.

q2.question: [TODO] Does it support hybrid facilitation?
q2.answer: [TODO] Yes. In-room and remote participants share the same join code, the same question flow, and the same view.

q3.question: [TODO] Can we export workshop outcomes quickly?
q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Structured outputs are available immediately and can be shared before participants leave the room.

training.headline: Check understanding while you still have the room.
training.subheadline: Run formative knowledge checks, confidence surveys, and reflection questions mid-session. Find out what participants actually understood — not what they said they understood.

passive.title: [TODO] Engagement looks fine, comprehension isn't
passive.desc: [TODO] Cameras stay on and heads stay up while the actual concept lands with one third of the cohort.

silence.title: [TODO] Learners don't ask in public
silence.desc: [TODO] Public-question formats favour the confident learners and leave gaps invisible until the assessment.

q1.question: [TODO] Can learners join without creating accounts?
q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. Learners join with a session code in the browser — no install, no account, no waiting room.

q2.question: [TODO] Can trainers run checks during modules?
q2.answer: [TODO] Yes. Trainers control timing — reveal, pause, and close each check based on the room's pace.

q3.question: [TODO] Can we use outcomes in L&D reporting?
q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Structured outputs feed Kirkpatrick L1 (reaction) and L2 (learning) summaries for program review.
